The '''72nd British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[BAFTAs]], were held on 10 February 2019 at the [[Royal Albert Hall]] in [[London]], honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2018 in film|2018]]. Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2018.

The nominees were announced on 9 January 2019 by actor [[Will Poulter]] and actress [[Hayley Squires]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Final Entered Films List 2018/19 |url=http://awards.bafta.org/sites/default/files/images/british_academy_film_awards_201819-enteredfilmslist.pdf |website=bafta.org |publisher=British Academy of Film and Television Arts |access-date=26 January 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://metro.co.uk/2019/01/09/will-poulter-looks-relaxed-2019-bafta-nominations-quitting-social-media-mental-health-8325510/ |title=Will Poulter looks relaxed at 2019 Bafta nominations after quitting social media over Bandersnatch reaction |date=9 January 2019 |work=[[Metro (British newspaper)|Metro]] |access-date=9 January 2019}}</ref> The period comedy-drama ''[[The Favourite (2018 film)|The Favourite]]'' received the most nominations: twelve across eleven categories.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-46807535 |title=Bafta Film Awards 2019: Olivia Colman's The Favourite leads nominations |date=9 January 2019 |work=[[BBC News]] |access-date=9 January 2019}}</ref>

English actress [[Joanna Lumley]] hosted the ceremony for the second consecutive year.<ref name="BAFTA 2019">{{cite web |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/ee-british-academy-film-awards-venue-and-host-announced-for-2019 |title=EE British Academy Film Awards: Venue and host announced for 2019 |date=2 January 2019 |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] |access-date=2 January 2019}}</ref>

The nominees were announced on 9 January 2019.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2019/jan/09/the-full-list-of-nominations-for-the-baftas-2019 |title=The full list of nominations for the Baftas 2019 |date=9 January 2019 |work=[[The Guardian]] |access-date=9 January 2019}}</ref> The winners were announced on 10 February 2019.<ref name="Winners">{{cite web |url=https://www.bafta.org/film/awards/ee-british-academy-film-awards-nominees-winners-2019 |title=EE British Academy Film Awards Winners in 2019 |date=10 February 2019 |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] |access-date=10 February 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/live/2019/feb/10/baftas-2019-the-red-carpet-the-ceremony-and-the-winners-live |title=Baftas 2019: Roma and The Favourite win big – as it happened |date=10 February 2019 |work=[[The Guardian]] |access-date=10 February 2019}}</ref>

On 6 February 2019, BAFTA announced they had suspended [[Bryan Singer]]'s name from ''[[Bohemian Rhapsody (film)|Bohemian Rhapsody]]''{{'}}s nomination for [[Outstanding British Film]] following [[Bryan Singer#Sexual assault allegations|accusations against Singer]] concerning [[sexual abuse]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2019/film/news/bafta-suspends-bryan-singer-bohemian-rhapsody-nomination-1203130262/ |title=BAFTA Suspends Bryan Singer's Nomination for 'Bohemian Rhapsody' |last=Clarke |first=Stewart |date=6 February 2019 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |access-date=6 February 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.theatlantic.com/magazine/archive/2019/03/bryan-singers-accusers-speak-out/580462/ |title='Nobody Is Going to Believe You' |last2=Potter |last1=French |first2=Maximillian |first1=Alex |date=23 January 2019 |work=[[The Atlantic]] |access-date=23 January 2019}}</ref>

==Ceremony information==
The ceremony took place at the [[Royal Albert Hall]] and was hosted by English actress [[Joanna Lumley]] for the second consecutive year.<ref name="BAFTA 2019" /> In a reference to the controversy surrounding [[Kevin Hart]]'s removal as host of the [[91st Academy Awards]], Lumley said that she suspects she probably would not have been hosting the ceremony if she were on [[Twitter]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.digitalspy.com/movies/a26274194/baftas-joanna-lumley-opening-speech-oscars-host-2019-kevin-hart/ |title=BAFTAs' Joanna Lumley throws shade at the Oscars hosting debacle |last=McCreesh |first=Louise |date=10 February 2019 |publisher=[[Digital Spy]] |access-date=10 February 2019}}</ref> It opened with a performance from [[Cirque du Soleil]] for the third consecutive year and was broadcast after a short delay on [[BBC One]].<ref name="BAFTA 2019" /><ref>{{cite web |url=https://metro.co.uk/2019/02/10/time-baftas-bbc-one-tonight-ceremony-shown-live-8503775/ |title=What time are the Baftas on BBC One tonight and is the ceremony shown live? |last=Westbrook |first=Caroline |date=10 February 2019 |work=[[Metro (British newspaper)|Metro]] |access-date=10 February 2019}}</ref>

The period comedy-drama ''[[The Favourite (2018 film)|The Favourite]]'' received the most nominations with twelve and won seven, including [[Outstanding British Film]], [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Leading Role|Best Actress in a Leading Role]] for [[Olivia Colman]], and [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Supporting Role|Best Actress in a Supporting Role]] for [[Rachel Weisz]].<ref name="Winners" /><ref>{{cite web |url=https://greekcitytimes.com/2019/02/11/yorgos-lanthimos-the-favourite-takes-home-seven-bafta-awards/ |title=Yorgos Lanthimos 'The Favourite' takes home seven BAFTA Awards |date=10 February 2019 |work=Greek City Times |access-date=10 February 2019}}</ref> ''[[Roma (2018 film)|Roma]]'' won four awards, including [[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]] and [[BAFTA Award for Best Direction|Best Director]] for [[Alfonso Cuarón]].<ref name="Winners" /> [[Rami Malek]] won [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Leading Role|Best Actor in a Leading Role]] for ''[[Bohemian Rhapsody (film)|Bohemian Rhapsody]]'', thanking [[Freddie Mercury]] in his acceptance speech.<ref name="Winners" /><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.irishexaminer.com/breakingnews/entertainment/rami-malek-thanks-freddie-mercury-as-he-wins-best-actor-bafta-903513.html |title=Rami Malek thanks Freddie Mercury as he wins best actor Bafta |date=10 February 2019 |work=[[Irish Examiner]] |access-date=10 February 2019}}</ref>

The ''In Memoriam'' section paid tribute to those who had died during the previous year, including [[Albert Finney]], [[William Goldman]], [[Penny Marshall]], [[Burt Reynolds]] and [[Nicolas Roeg]]. The segment was accompanied by saxophonist Jess Gillam, who performed "[[(Where Do I Begin?) Love Story]]", the title song from the film ''[[Love Story (1970 film)|Love Story]]'', as composed by [[Francis Lai]], who also was featured in the tribute.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.nwemail.co.uk/news/17424087.sax-star-jess-gillam-lifts-the-roof-at-the-baftas-at-the-royal-albert-hall/ |title=Sax star Jess Gillam lifts the roof at the BAFTA's at the Royal Albert Hall |last=Mullen |first=Adrian |date=11 February 2019 |work=[[The Mail (Cumbria)|The Mail]] |access-date=11 February 2019}}</ref> [[Thelma Schoonmaker]] won the [[BAFTA Fellowship]], presented by [[Cate Blanchett]] and [[Prince William]]. She joined the fellowship along with her late husband [[Michael Powell]] and long-term collaborator [[Martin Scorsese]].<ref name="Schoonmaker"/> [[Elizabeth Karlsen]] and [[Stephen Woolley]] won the [[BAFTA Outstanding British Contribution to Cinema Award]] for their contributions to [[Number 9 Films]].<ref name="N9F"/>

==Presenters==
* [[Melissa McCarthy]] presented Outstanding British Film<ref name="Presenters">{{cite web |url=https://www.goldderby.com/article/2019/2019-bafta-awards-host-presenters-nominees/ |title=2019 BAFTA Awards: Who is hosting and presenting, which nominees will (and won't) be there? |last=Sheehan |first=Paul |date=9 February 2019 |publisher=[[Gold Derby]] |access-date=9 February 2019}}</ref>
* [[Lily Collins]] and [[Olga Kurylenko]] presented Best Animated Film and Best Production Design<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Jason Isaacs]] presented Best British Short Animation<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Henry Golding]] and [[Eleanor Tomlinson]] presented Best Sound and Best Editing<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Lucy Boynton]] and [[Joseph Fiennes]] presented Best Documentary and Best Hair and Makeup<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Bill Nighy]] presented the BAFTA Outstanding British Contribution to Cinema Award<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Mary J. Blige]] and [[Elliot Page]] presented Best Actress in a Supporting Role<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Luke Evans]] and [[Regina King]] presented the EE Rising Star Award<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Yalitza Aparicio]] and [[Marina de Tavira]] presented Best Adapted Screenplay<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Danai Gurira]] and [[Andy Serkis]] presented Best Original Music<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Elizabeth Debicki]] and [[Michelle Rodriguez]] presented Best Original Screenplay<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Viola Davis]] presented Best Actor in a Supporting Role<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Will Poulter]] and [[Michelle Yeoh]] presented Best Cinematography<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Riz Ahmed]] and [[Rachel Brosnahan]] presented Outstanding Debut by a British Writer, Director or Producer<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Jamie Bell]], [[Taron Egerton]], and [[Richard Madden]] presented Best Special Visual Effects<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Thandie Newton]] and [[Sophie Okonedo]] presented Best Film Not in the English Language<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Cynthia Erivo]] and [[Eddie Marsan]] presented Best Costume Design<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Salma Hayek]] presented Best Director<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Gary Oldman]] presented Best Actress in a Leading Role<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Margot Robbie]] presented Best Actor in a Leading Role<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Chiwetel Ejiofor]] presented Best Film<ref name="Presenters" />
* [[Cate Blanchett]] and [[Prince William]] presented the BAFTA Fellowship<ref name="Presenters" />
